text:
Hunter College - overall student ratings on RateMyProfessors.
Overall Quality: 3.0
Safety: 3.8
Location: 3.7
Reputation: 3.5
Internet: 3.0
Opportunities: 3.0
Happiness: 3.0
Clubs: 2.8
Facilities: 2.7
Social: 2.4
Food: 2.4

Security guards are great however the profs for the comp sci program are ATROCIOUS, multiple fail the final fail the course classes makes you wonder why even come to class, crowded in the library and the walkways around 12pm. No areas to study and loud in the library 4th and 5th floor despite being quiet floors. not enough academic opportunities.

The school is okay i guess, the computer science program is ATROCIOUS, multiple fail the final fail the course classes that make you question why even come to class then if your grade is determined on two exams. Overcrowded in the library, a bunch of larpers, not enough spaces to do work for a school that has like 18+ floors in multiple buildings.

Ceilings are falling on students while admin stays silent. Racism is tolerated. Allison Friedman remains suspended instead of fired despite clear evidence. The School of Education is a joke. They missed the decision deadline and the office is a ghost town. No updates, no professionalism. They only care about your money, not your safety or future.

I liked my professors here (for the most part), but the communication aspect has A LOT of room for improvement. Emails from departments (financial aid, academic advising, etc) were often unclear and even from some of my professors! Social life is ok and it is possible to connect, but overall this experience was aggravating and frustrating!

A school, at the bare minimum, is supposed to be a place you can go and study. Hunter can't even get this right. The facilities are an overcrowded disaster. Hope you like asbestos! People are catching on that it's possible to coast to a degree with ChatGPT and learn nothing. Diplomas from here with be worthless in five years.

This campus is great if you want to be in and out for your classes only. It's a HORRIFIC place to stay any longer than you have to. The student body is either made of fashionistas who care too much and judge you, or the most boring people who don't speak. Not a great place to make friends or connections.
great school with so m ay majors

The school is located in the Upper East Side, where delis and restaurants often raise their prices even if the quality is low. Elevators in the North Building can take up to 10 minutes to arrive, requiring you to leave for class earlier. Around noon, it gets hectic, especially at the intersection between buildings on the 3rd floor.

I had the lowest expectations and maybe that is why I was very shocked once I started. I've made some really good friends, the department of my major has helped me a lot to grow in my field and looking for jobs. Not all departments are great, but overall most faculty will try to help you not fail. Honestly I like it.

Hunter is what you make it. If you don't like people, if you don't go to events, if you don't talk in GCs, if you don't join any clubs...you will hate your time here. Everyone has the chance to find their people, but you may have to leave your comfort zone to do it. Food is meh and $$$. Make friends with security! Take advantage of the resources.

This is my undergrad year, I was very hyped to go to hunter college, the transition is very major i'll say, I noticed off the back with most of the fall semesters some professors dont gaf about how stressful it is to keep up, the biggest thing you'll hear is "go to office hours" . If you started here I wish you luck , i'm literally graying early !
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
Do not come here unless you have no other options. The worst CUNY by far
I mean it's a cuny what could possibly go wrong.

Hunter College is a good school just for the name, meaning reputation for a higher paying job. As far as attending, most of the Professor they hire are under qualified because they lack diversity, communication, patience, and understanding with their students. They're hired based on education, but not their character. Not an excellent school.
I started this semester and right now it been a really good experience.
One of the better Cuny colleges out there

Honestly this school isn't great academically. Im not in the nursing program yet and it already caused me several panic attacks, stress and caused me to cry out of stress. the advisors get paid for nothing lol
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
I'm in my senior year. If you come here looking for a good academic programs, I'm sorry to tell you, Hunter is not good academically.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.

Yea overrated. Improvements really needed, problems: crowded, low seating/spaces/class registrations. Many professors can teach poorly; learn to self teach, make friends (you can, be proactive, pls have faith in yourself and others) In short: it's just not it, but you just got to endure it and comfort yourself by the cost;
Science courses are hard, but not impossible. Very low social life, but good internet (still don't take exams/quizzes at campus if you can avoid it).

I attended Hunter for 2 years, and transferred out before Covid hit. If I hadn't, I'm not sure if I would have graduated with high marks due to the fact that it seems as if most professors here would rather you stroke their ego in the work instead of staying objective. I agree with other commenters on the commons area, shotty buildings, etc.
Its Overall an awesome school, and great for the price your paying if any, and I would recommend it to anyone.

This school has great opportunities for students planning to pursue careers related to their studies (I.e., internships 8 and networking). However, the facility could use some work as the environment is dull and can get depressing at times. As a science major, science classes here tend to be unnecessarily hard, but the school has great resources.

I feel that, as a graduate student, my experience is much different than most have at Hunter. But honestly, having gone to a private university for undergrad, I wish I could have done it at Hunter instead. Professors are brilliant, the CUNY system is solid, and I'm not paying $100k like I did with my BA. It's a degree, just get in and get out.

Im currently a junior majoring in psych, you will be on your own in nearly everything.There is little to no help when it comes to future planning. You will be alone most of the time if you dont FORCE yourself into everything, and it shouldnt be. its an okay school, but does not live up to the name its given. please be thorough when deciding.

Advisors mislead you a lot. You are forced to take unnecessary classes and the worst dealing with rudness of people in the office. Nobody graduates on time, some people sit on the floor during classes because no seats, impossible to register for class. If you want a degree fast and affordable this is not the option.
I would have never came here if I knew how much it sucked. so many people build a rep for this school and then when you actually see it, it's a shame. I hate it here
Don't come here if you don't have a scholarship and even if you do have a scholarship, second-guess yourself before enrolling at Hunter. Everything is wack about this school. Trust the ratings.

psych major. i acted as my own counselor & graduated early w a double minor. for psych majors, there are so many opportunities. you can get amazing internships. important to connect w professors & psych peers. for social life, i met many friends through language courses & clubs. you need to actively pursue friendships bc everyone commutes.

Construction is always occurring; the school needs fixing inside all over, and most professors are mid. If you are looking for the "college experience," do not attend. There is no social life. The SEEK program is good, and the school's reputation is decent.

SO much construction near and in the school, but nothing ever looks updated. Food is ehh and expensive, taste like middle school food. Only thing good about the school is the USG ( they provide lots of activities) and Public safety ( safe school, and good teamwork, they make lots of outreach events). Classrooms are super outdated, small and hot.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.

While the location is good and the subway is legit right outside it's extremely academically rigorous to a fault. Wish I had known that, creates a super overwhelming environment, where everyone is trying to catch up on work and there isn't much socialization. Professors are hit or miss, most being the former. Ended up teaching myself everything.

Actually, this school isn't what the public said it represents. Yes, it does have great professors but some of them aren't great either. Always an issue with the electric stairs, very crowded hallways, and they place you in such big classrooms to only grade you based on exams. Is very hard to make friends. And most of the time you will be alone.

I regret it. Don't go here unless all you want is a degree. You won't have much of a social life. Most classes are academically rigorous, even when completely unnecessary. Painfully disorganized, anything involving bureaucracy will make you question your faith in humanity. Overcrowded. Building is in shambles, very few places for quiet study. Sad.

This school wasn't what I expected it might be. Some professors are great while others are not really great at teaching. The main campus is still under construction and even some places look old and in need of a renovation. Their financial aid and bursar's office don't really help you, all they ask you to do is to email someone else.
It was an okay school, wasn't there for very long but it was okay

Queens College - overall student ratings on RateMyProfessors.
Overall Quality: 3.2
Safety: 3.8
Facilities: 3.5
Reputation: 3.5
Location: 3.5
Happiness: 3.3
Opportunities: 3.2
Clubs: 3.1
Internet: 3.0
Social: 2.8
Food: 2.7

Hi, so if you're thinking about coming here, the people are super cool, and the kids are okay if you find your people here. However, the staff and faculty here have the highest 50/50 rate of getting the best professor ever or the worst prof ever. The school's help center is even worse. Please visit the school and do research before going here.
It's an okay school. Not much of a social life here but there's decent foods, decent buildings, and decent professors. It's an okay school to just go to get your education and nothing else.
Very obviously underfunded but you get what you make of it.

It is difficult to make friends here because most people are to themselves, and the fact that this is a commuter school doesn't make it any better. There are limited good food options in the area and none on campus. They have poor communication, especially when it comes to upcoming campus events. There is almost no school spirit. Transferring now
Best school ever for best future life
I genuienly regret coming here and I can't wait to transfer

Everyone that I have met, students and staff, are always friendly. The atmosphere is great and there is always a place to go to if you need someplace quiet to study or a place to go to if you need and help in studying.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.

Please listen to the negative reviews and AVOID THIS SCHOOL IF YOU WANT TO MAJOR IN STEM!!! It's almost as if the professors here want to keep you in based on how much they can't teach, are unhelpful, and are EXTREMELY HARSH GRADERS regardless of how much you try. Do yourself a favor and enroll into a better school that actually cares about STEM!!

I came here only because it was close to my house. It was not my first choice and I sincerely hope that anyone majoring in computer science does not come here. Worst Professors in the CS departments. They do not hire capable professors who can at least communicate properly. Every semester there should be individuals to judge if they doing their job

Queens College was alright, good school to go to for convenience and distance, decent undergrad programs, nothing special. Solid stepping stone for grad school. Location and advisors are good, professors are hit and miss, social life is not great. They also undersell the resources they have, try and explore the campus on an off day.

I'm about to complete a full semester and have learned that the school is what you make it. Stay up to date on your emails for career readiness/internships/part-time positions, follow the school's social media to learn about monthly events, and look into clubs before club day to get an idea of what route you want to go. A really beautiful campus.
Fairly average, to be honest. Attend if it's close to home, but otherwise, you might want to consider other options.
School is great for art and for queens residents.

Great sociology and philosophy classes. The professors here are really great, and the people at QC are super accommodating as long as you are there to learn which should be the point of your education. The classes are super fun and are really interesting if you try and immerse yourself into them. I highly recommend!
Great school for the money! I loved it.Great professors.
Just going into the accounting program and become an accountant to make 85k first year post-grad

The point of college is to get a high paying career out of it with as little debt as possible and high ROI. However, this college offers very little guidance. The career center, a very important facility, is extremely underfunded. It is hard to put your foot in the door and hard to get classes you need. You need to really know what career you want.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
It's a great school. Really wonderful professors.
lots of food options but theyre constantly changing. they have a few Halal as well. nothing Kosher yet
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.

Best way to make way is through clubs. There have been a lot of changes to what and where you can eat. Food trucks are now on campus by the quad, and the student center has good stuff. I can't speak for all of them; professors depend mainly on the department.

The negative comments are going to say what they say, but you get out of QC exactly what you put in. If you're not the type to take time to study by yourself this is not for you. There are some great professors here that will go out of their way to help you if you ask, but there's also bad ones. You just have to know how to navigate real life.
Good school, especially for education majors. Only thing is there is too much traffic close to campus, but hey, it's the city.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.

they're tuition-farming here at QC (passing most 100-200-level classes so students get to the 300-level where they will almost certainly retake an indefinite number of times i.e. sunk cost). No resources whatsoever for CS majors, they will fail you for the SLIGHTEST error the minute you hit 300-level classes bc they know you'll retake. STAY AWAY.

Barely feels like I even go here. The professors give nothing in terms of guidance, help, etc. Complete lack of even the bare minimum from the Comp Sci dept to interact with/help students. Brightspace is a DISASTER, most professors don't even bother posting exercises/material most of the time so none of us even know what to do. Do not enroll here.
It's not too bad. Some departments are better than others.
Get better profs for some CS classes and QCCS should be highly ranked.
The best school to go to in NY
I DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS SCHOOL TO ANY STUDENT IF YOU NEED HELP IN ACADEMIC. YOU CAN DO BETTER WITH ANY OTHER SCHOOL! WORSE DECISION EVER TO GET INTO THIS SCHOOL.

Do not go here, they do not help you, they pretend like your best friend while enrolled but don't care two hoots about you thereafter. Going to right a proper review on a couple professors in the nutrition and exercise sciences program soon
do not go for stem, FNES, or any health profession major. It is good for majors in arts and education.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
This school is basically a scam, at least the program I was in was...
Great for a cheap art education!

If you are looking for an art school that doesn't cost an unreasonable amount of money, chose Queens College. The professors are incredible knowledgable and work in the field in NYC which is an epicenter of art. There is an art community which is stellar for artistic development. Queens College Art Department has over delivered.

Baruch College - overall student ratings on RateMyProfessors.
Overall Quality: 3.4
Location: 4.2
Safety: 4.1
Reputation: 4.0
Opportunities: 3.8
Happiness: 3.4
Clubs: 3.3
Internet: 3.2
Facilities: 3.1
Social: 2.8
Food: 2.8

Great school for many career paths. Met some amazing humanities professors with impressive backgrounds and true passion for what they do. Naturally, some professors aren't the best (as is the case w/ every college ever) but they don't outweigh the opportunities, environment, and safety of the school that you get from CUNY. Highly recommend.
Outstanding and affordable education

Worst CUNY college ever. The most mismanaged college. There are some great professors but some of the professors are bunch of clowns. The departments like ISSC, office of graduate studies and office of registrar. Absolutely worst departments.
Good school for business or accounting. Great location almost midtown. The closest thing u have for a cafeteria are microwaves shared among thousands.

What's up with the lack of SACC appointments for intermediate and advanced economics? Is this Baruch College? The best public business school in the northeast or what? These are some of the most challenging classes in the major, yet supportive resources seem limited.

As it relates to master's programs, I have enjoyed the diversity and professionalism of the student cohort, the many programs and events available, the faculty and the campus location. It is a great institution and definitely a great investment!

Baruch is a respectable and known school, they offer alot of opportunities, and theres alot of networking events here.It is a antisocial school, so you have to make the best and put yourself out there. Join clubs ! A big chunk of the professors are not good like the elevators here , and some prof are really good. Goodluck and just keep going !

The classes are packed, so not much class participation is encouraged. Many profs recommend using ChatGPT since they don't want to bother teaching students how to write on their own. And it seems that the college admin does not think textbooks are important since it's pushing "open educational resources."
very good school if you are interested in business
If you're an introvert, you're cooked
the College is good but advisors should be a little more helpful maybe students need to be assigned to an specific one to have an easier school life.

Great for accounting and finance, you get what you pay for it. Clubs however are not the best, you are better going out of the city for that. Networking and opportunities are better here though. Be mindful that this is a commuter school so it's rare for anyone to stick around after classes.
The school serves students who are purposeful and goal-oriented.
Very good choice for students pursuing a career in accounting.

You do have to go out of your way to get answers when it comes to your schedule or things like that. But I feel like every CUNY college is like that. The school is fun is you're willing to interact with others.
This is a very good school but it makes me so miserable I can't believe there is a chance for me to ever live a happy life.
This school offers a variety of experiences. Their are various events to attend. They offer free tutoring and even have a great writing center.
Very cool school. Lots of opportunities and backgrounds to connect with.
A lot of the professors just read off the slides. There are limited tutoring sessions for upper-level classes and limited office hours.

In my experience, Baruch is a great college. The faculty and opportunities are outstanding. The location could not be easier to get to. The internet worked every time I logged on. I think they could improve the amount of seating and study areas as well as improve the quality of the gym by keeping it orderly and clean. Final grade on the school: A-

Baruch, being a CUNY has many flaws. In addition to being a city college, there is a lack of the social aspect in typical colleges. However, one thing it doesn't lack, is the opportunities. The professors are one thing, but the clubs offer a great headstart to break into the finance industry. If you do your due diligence you will thrive.

It's an okay school. The building does need a lot of work and more seating areas. Heat needs to be turned on in winter because some of the classrooms are very cold. Also, professors got to step up their game. Most of them are just reading off slides.

Baruch College excels in business education through its prestigious Zicklin School of Business, affordability, and prime location in Manhattan. It offers students access to internships and networking opportunities in a diverse and vibrant environment. However, it has limited campus life as a commuter school and high program competition.
Transferring here was probably the better idea. Good school and helps a ton with networking. Professors actually want you to pass and I havent really had a bad experience. Good school/

I graduated Baruch undergrad (accounting) with no debt and an job at a big four firm. I recommend this school for anyone who is self driven and knows how to manage their time and work well. Don't expect much else from the school. The faculty is a mixed bag. Do your due diligence and you will thrive.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.

If you are a hard worker, and you don't care too much about the social side of the college experience, Baruch is a 5/5. In business, law, and related fields, you will find that making connections and getting work will be much easier than any other non-ivy school.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
Very bad facilities, they are depressing

The quality of education is good. It's worth coming here if you are already working and need to finish a degree. Otherwise, I suggest going to a college with better resources. I know that the job market is extremely difficult right now, but it is clear that the top NYC employers simply do not respect/recognize the non-accounting programs.

Poorly funded and we don't get dedicated supplementary TA sessions even for a large course unlike Columbia. Good professors seem busy and they don't bother to care individual students. I guess we get what we pay for.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
I mean the school is alright. You get what you pay for I guess.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
The science department here is a joke, majority of the science professors suck and the good ones barely teach there

Baruch is a great school but you need to grindddddd. If your ambitions and career oriented this is perfect, make use of the STARR events and join clubs. It's a great school in a location where you can do lot of internship during the semester.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.

Columbia University - overall student ratings on RateMyProfessors.
Overall Quality: 3.8
Reputation: 4.7
Location: 4.4
Opportunities: 4.3
Internet: 3.9
Facilities: 3.9
Happiness: 3.7
Clubs: 3.6
Safety: 3.5
Social: 3.4
Food: 3.4

What an incredible experience. My profs were all great and I really appreciate the one who taught me all about Mr Sidney Poitier (you know who you are!)! When I get on the big screen I'm thanking you, Columbia!!!
reputation, quality of internet, and safety have taken a major nosedive recently ...
Online students beware. You'll pay the same tution but won't have the same access to courses, leadership opportunities, mentorship, or social engagement.
One of the best universities in the country, right in Manhattan. Doesn't get much better.
University is wonderful and will provide all that you give into it. Relatively stressful, but you will survive with simple time management. All in all, great university.
It's Columbia University, excellent caliber.
This academic institution is immaculate and overall a very inspirational and eye opening experience.
Great university with in theory good alumni connections. However, this is being eroded rapidly.
definitely have to take the good with the bad with ivies.

This is the worst academic experience I have ever had. Community college and a 2 year state school would've been a much better experience. The professors are mean, and horrid at teaching, and the stress pollutes the are that you breathe. We have 1-2 suicides every semester, usually off of the roof of the dorms and straight into the grounds.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.

The school is ambitious in its offerings - thus has a boatload of opportunities. Mostly STEM-oriented school, though, so writing support is limited to ENG majors. Research is abundant; clubs are diverse and easy to join. Classes - fun. Food is decadent and the community is welcoming and quiet. Admissions focus is on community leaders and it shows.
I enjoyed this school a lot! Even though it may be tough to get into, I do recommend trying out for it because it ends up giving you a lot of opportunities.

Going here is like buying Gucci. People know what it is, people know it's expensive, and certain people will let you into their circle because you're wearing it. That said, these flip flops are making my feet bleed, this sweater is made out of plastic and it's digging into my collarbone, and this hat is disintegrating. Don't come here to be happy.
The stress culture is next level.

The worst thing about Columbia is the same thing about every competitive school. Many of your classmates lack basic social skills and have little understanding of the world. You just need to step back and relax your ego. Open yourself up to others as much as possible.
A very bureaucratic organization with only obedience
An overrated school filled with master students.

In STEM Professors don't know how to teach. They either read slides or do performative math in lectures. The physics department is run like an amateur clown show; they did not tell students at they needed to be registered for the recitation section directly linked with the lecture section until half way through the semester.

All the students are very dedicated here which makes it incredibly hard to stay above the mean, most classes are curved to a B+. Facilities, including the libraries and gym, are always over crowded. Also not safe in the area, a student was murdered last week less than two blocks from campus and I am constantly receiving notification of robberies.
I loved coming to Columbia for my Master's degree. Great reputation and jobs are more readily available to me because of this. I loved all of my professors and classmates!
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
I just joined their MBA program. Very friendly and organized, especially for someone like me flying in and out every other week for classes.
:))) Best choice I've ever made.

Columbia is a prestigious, well-placed school. It's definitely expensive, so that's something to think about. Expect a lot of work, but you'll eventually get used to it and the opportunities are amazing if you're comfortable with reaching out. Some of my favorite researchers and scientists in high school are now my professors.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
Hate attending this school and so do a decent amount of students here but none have the guts to transfer because of the name it carries. I should have went to a CUNY school :(
Don't expect real teachers, its a research institution
Some pros and cons, work is stressful but also definitely a work hard party hard culture around a lot of the campus.

A really great university with a very nice location in the middle of concrete jungle. There are a lot of options to meet, mingle and talk. Dept. of Chemistry is straightforward and nice place to be. However everybody is expected to work hard, and it is really a place to go. Tuition is extremely high as well as living costs are among highest...

Advising could be improved and there is too much interdepartmental drama between administration and non-tenured staff and graduate TAs. Students suffer because of lack of continuity among staff and coursework. Excellent opportunities post graduation. On campus recruitment, A+++ professors, off campus eats and good social life.

Binghamton - overall student ratings on RateMyProfessors.
Overall Quality: 3.8
Reputation: 4.1
Clubs: 3.9
Happiness: 3.9
Social: 3.8
Opportunities: 3.8
Safety: 3.8
Facilities: 3.8
Internet: 3.7
Location: 3.1
Food: 3.0

You need to experience Binghamton to understand how good of a school it is. There are so many opportunities at the school, you just have to put in the time to figure it out. Food on campus is not good.

If you want to take undergraduate courses, but already have a bachelors degree, then you will be forced to enroll as a graduate student and have to pay graduate level tuition which is 50% more than undergraduate tuition. Admissions won't tell you this, it's not expressly written anywhere, and you won't find out until you get slapped with the bill.

I think this school offers many opportunities and resources, but its location is its main downfall. I feel that there isn't much to do off campus, and it's upstate, so it's pretty underdeveloped and many crack heads.
Great school, lots of fun to be had if you actively look for it; the worst parts by far are the location and food.

This is the most affordable "public ivy" education that I have been able to find. I will say a lot of these bad reviews are because Bing was a lot of ivy-league hopefuls' safety school, so its naturally not going to live up to those expectations. We just replaced our President, and things are on the up-swing including the food (THANK GOD).
I'm only a sophomore here, but the school is only going to grow with time- hop on the train before overlooking it when applying to schools.
Great school, amazing amenities, and great support. Graduated from SOM. Definitely make use of resources there.
There's no support for business majors, only accounting students. If you're not in accounting, it's hard navigating everything. The professors also encourage Accounting here, which is unfortunate

Good school with decent opportunities, but the location is not ideal - it's far from major cities. Crime rate around the area is higher than I'd like, and I do not recommend going downtown alone at night for safety reasons. This is something to consider when choosing this school.
love it here. bad food. great people. good clubs and social life.
The food, for now, really does suck. But otherwise I really do love it here, campus is gorgeous and everything is relatively new. The construction does stink, but otherwise no complaints
i love binghamton but the food is just so bad. so many good research opportunities and i have a ton of close friends :)
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
still enrolled, love the place and people. feels like education is still valued

WOW the food is bad. I don't know why it's so high when it failed 7/8 food quality checks for the past 5 years and it's hard to find chicken that is not raw in C4. Otherwise, I think it is a great university in terms of opportunities, safety, and working areas. Libraries are open and helpful, good amount of nice people and staff, 8/10 accessibility
If you are coming from a more urban environment, there will definitely be some adjusting.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
love it here but the food lowk sucks

Binghamton grew on me. Academics, programs, and social life are all great. My dorm situation has been great, but some folks have had less of a good experience. My main complaints are that admins aren't really responsive, and the food options and preparation are sub-par at best. Overall, great for the price, especially if you plan on a grad degree.

I would say the class can challenging, either being the subject or the professors. But you will find plenty of social and academic opportunities here. Additionally, I think there are alot of interesting things here.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.
Good school, foods mid but definitely manageable. Not too bad socially, but definitely wish the school had more school spirit, I think the fact there's no football team really takes away from that

Lots of clubs to choose from and there's a decently big party scene off campus. Definitely not the safest campus or town. Do not walk around at night by yourself. Food is mid but edible. Hard to keep up if you fall behind for a few days. You will be burnt out and tired even in the first month of your semester.

I would say that it offer plenty of opportunities for academic and carrer growth, but be careful with social life because some people are not meant to be friends. Said the same thing about any of the clubs and especially any student union or organization they can be picky
This is one of the best places to go to if you want a social life. There are so many community events, clubs with numerous activities, you will never run out of things to do.
GREAT public school - worth the money considering the in-state tuition

I'm a recent grad from Binghamton and I loved my time there! I did a complete 180, I went from absolutely hating it, to eventually finding a really solid community of people! The people really do make the place & I wouldn't trade my experience for the world. I loved my psych & edu profs, and greek life really gave me a community I needed.
This university has lots of opportunities for students to explore and find their place but I would have to be honest that it has it's downside
It's a nice school outside of the big cities. Everyone is super friendly and it's a safe campus to be on. I also has a nice nature preserve behind the campus, easy to get to shopping locations.
This rating has been removed as per our site guidelines.

This is the best college I have ever gone to.
Stony Brook is one of the best schools I have attended, the instructors and staff are amazing. Food choices should be better, specifically for vegetarians.

Graduate Alumni speaking - SBU is a great school! The undergraduate population might complain - but they haven't seen other schools to compare. SBU will give you a great education at a very reasonable price compared to schools outside SUNY. Huge plus to have a school be attached to a great hospital as well!

I came here from a different country, only knowing that the school was highly ranked. So far, I've had very good opportunities presented to me and the food is okay. Outdoors is great outside winter. Generally people are happy. Not a lot of room to grow, but if you're already good, it's great place to do well. Very unforgiving environment.

Coming from someone who transferred from another elite school, Cornell University is unique in how thoroughly it can prepare you for the next steps after college. Even in a tough professional job market my friends and I have secured top jobs by virtue of Cornell's network.

I transferred here as a soph. Incredible research opportunities and great food compared to most colleges. Ithaca isn't for everyone, but I love being in a collegetown with public transport. Location is a big one make sure u like being a campus school

Honestly location is bad viewed in the perspective of getting in or out of Ithaca. If we only look at how the area is, then it's not bad at all. Most people like to diss on Cornell, but it's a great school. I have had some great professors, but grade deflation is very real here so don't expect an easy A. Also, lots of entitled rich students, which is definitely a change from coming from a poor, rural area in the south. I have never felt unsafe walking across campus at 2am as a short woman.
It's a great school but not for everyone. Do your research before coming.

Fantastic by almost all metrics: academics, faculty, reputation, opportunities, and yes, even location (Ithaca, while remote, is stunning). And unlike that safety school in Boston, there's no grade inflation! 10/10.

The course are pretty good, you can learn many knowledge in the course. Many professors are very nice (except my advisor), and also very famous in academia. It's in the middle of nowhere, but otherwise, Cornell is an amazing school that will benefit you for the rest of your life.

top tier school, wouldn't really want to be anywhere else. only complaints are that the work is very tough (compared to other ivies), and ithaca is sorta wack and in the middle of nowhere. overall beautiful campus and great academic/career opportunities

Asinine school with overcrowded classes, misleading class description. E permits takes long time to process if you're an engineering major. pay for a different major

you will learn theory, no numbers but only proofs... spend hours learning and be tested on something totally different or which is not taught on the book....Save Your Money, pay for a different major. you will graduate earlier in 4 years and probably get to work earlier. otherwise stay there for almost 6-8 years.

CCNY Engineering relies on hostile bottlenecks and scheduling traps. When students use ePermits to graduate on time, the administration actively blocks them. I survived by routing courses through four campuses, but the system is built to break you

overcrowded classes, misleading class description. E permits take long time to process if you're an engineering major, considering you can't even enroll because classes are full. Job prospects for 90% of majors are mta and con ed

Trying to make progress toward my Electrical engineering degree & several required electives weren't not available through advising plan I was given. When I raised concerns, I didn't not feel they were addressed in a constructive way. Overall, the experience negatively affected my academic planning.

Rest in peace if you're doing electrical engineering, anyone taking ee205 but needs to take ee306 is getting screwed due to EE combine ee205/306 as 1 class (program change). If you're still into EE, transfer to a better school in SUNY. I only recommend CCNY by taking engineering courses at cuny price. Save yourself frustration & getting screwed

Professors don't care about what they teach, care even less about what you learn, are chronic over-sharers, and multiple professors have talked about which students will make it. It is a bad environment no matter your field but at least the connections you make with the other students can be good.

If you're serious about engineering and computer science, pick your choices carefully. It may be worth to make it a sacrifice to go to a better school in the SUNY. Engineering here is dysfunctional and you will need to self study a lot here.

Science courses feel very challenging, with grading & exam expectations sometimes feeling inconsistent. Students often rely heavily on peers & self-study due to limited structured support. Workload & environment can feel very stressful/demanding. students may feel participation or subjective factors play in final outcomes which can be frustrating

Student support services can feel inconsistent at times, and responses to concerns may vary. Navigating administrative processes sometimes requires persistence and self-advocacy. Students should be prepared to be proactive when seeking help or information.

CCNY & Grove was a difficult experience as a STEM student. I often had to rely heavily on self-study due to limited instructional support & infrequent course offerings. Resources & career guidance felt limited, & graduating on time was challenging. Students should plan early internships & be prepared for a highly independent learning environment.

My experience at Grove school of engineering was very challenging. I went from honors to dismissal due to low GPA. Exams often didn't match lectures, classes filled fast, and support felt limited. Save your money—consider a different major to graduate on time, or go elsewhere if you're serious about engineering.

engineering here may work well for students who are very comfortable with theoretical material & independent learning. However, if you prefer structured teaching, applied work, or more guided support, you may want to consider other options. Hard to enroll in required classes. Many filled up quickly & getting permission is challenge.

CCNY is a 3rd world country, professors and people that run grove school are unreasonable and engineering programs are disorganized. Rest in peace if you're an engineering student, save yourself constant frustration & advisors never help which means you have to figure things out yourself, dysfunctional college I ever attended. Save your money

Save yourself from getting screwed by this college. City College of NY is cheap, but the experience reflects it. Have fun trying to get ePermits especially if you're an engineering major—which is one of the only ways to survive here. It's inconsistent, competitive, and not something you can rely on. Pay for a different major to graduate on time.

I've noticed the computer science program is run by unreasonable professors who don't seem willing to help students. When I asked an advisor about classes, I was given options that were full or not offered, and the response was essentially “take it or leave it,” which felt dismissive and frustrating.

I have seen A cycle that can delay graduation & makes it hard to plan ahead. It feels like poor coordination between departments, leaving students stuck trying to make schedules work. This school really needs to improve how prerequisites & required courses are scheduled so students can progress without unnecessary setbacks

most frustrating issues I've experienced at CCNY is inconsistent course availability, especially with prerequisites. required class isn't offered because pre-req isn't available that semester. Then, after finally completing pre-req, actual class needed for your degree isn't offered following term.

There's poor coordination with prerequisites and required courses. You can't take a class because the prereq isn't offered, then after completing it, the needed course isn't available. This delays graduation and creates unnecessary stress.

Course availability is really inconsistent. Sometimes required classes aren't offered because the prereqs aren't available. Then once you finish the prereq, the class you actually need disappears next semester. It makes planning your degree frustrating.

Professor don't care about students, can't teach, and show favoritism. in some programs courses are offered once a year not once a semester. Finally, engineering school technology is very weak compared to other schools. (Other schools have labs for the equipment we see on the textbooks only).

I transferred here from UNLV following the shooting in 2023 and I regret it everyday. I genuinely would rather have my mind wiped daily, and then have to relive the shooting every single day as a new memory, than go back to CCNY ever again.

I learned the hard way that google reviews about CCNY's engineering program weren't exaggerating. There often aren't enough seats in required classes, & some professors are unreasonable/focus heavily on theory instead of practical applications. Save yourself from getting screwed over constantly & consider going somewhere else.

Rest in peace if you're an engineering student at City College of New York. Administration doesn't allocate enough funding to the engineering school, so required classes often don't have enough seats. Combined with some unreasonable or ineffective professors, it can make the experience very frustrating. You save yourself being constantly screwed

The electrical engineering undergraduate program here is very dysfunctional with confusing electives ee342/ee371. Save your money by paying for a different major for the sake of graduating on time & you're not in school forever. Save yourself from screwed by grove. if you're serious about engineering, go to a better school then here.
Engineering should have shorter summer classes then 8 weeks.

After the third year, I transferred out to another school after a negative experience with engineering program at Grove. I can only describe what I experience in grove school, my advice is save your money pay for a different major for the sake of graduating on time. CCNY will give you stress, nightmares and PTSD

overcrowded classes, misleading class description, & bad science department. Epermits take long time to process, which are required if you're an engineering major, considering you can't register for your preferred time schedule because classes are full. Job prospects for 90% of majors are poor after graduation.

Was a Biology major, and it was the worst decision of my life. Had to figure out everything on your own with no help. Classes were so hard to pass. Professors were forced to teach. That's how they got research funding. Test avgs were in the 30-40s. Had to curve a lot for kids to pass. Academics screwed me over, and I had to stay an extra year.
Academics are great, others are meh
This institution and grove school system will give you Selective mutism
I recommend double-checking all advising information yourself. If something goes wrong, advising blame problems on student. so be very careful and double-check everything yourself.

Honestly, he's not a bad professor, but it can be hard to understand him sometimes. He doesn't repeat himself if you need extra help he will tell you to check online. Overall though, he's really nice and actually very funny, which makes class more enjoyable.

This is where academic dreams die. Everyone that works here from professors to admin offices wants to see everyone fail. They enjoy making everything hard on students because they can. Nobody smiles or cares to make an impact or be kind. Everyday I regret choosing this CUNY school over maritime or buffalo state College. Biggest mistake of my life.

Its great school but the lack of staff and good teachers makes life so more challenging for no reason. with the right professors, you learn hard topics easily mean while with a weird one, you just go with the flow and fail even after trying so hard.

If you're considering engineering or computer science, weigh your options carefully. It may be worth making financial or logistical sacrifices to attend a stronger SUNY program. The CUNY system, especially for STEM, is in serious disarray.

My time at CCNY was one of the worst periods of my life. For STEM students in particular, I strongly urge you not to buy the hype. environment is extremely cutthroat, and there is little sense of community—most students are simply trying to survive.

If you're considering engineering or computer science, weigh your options carefully. It may be worth making financial or logistical sacrifices to attend a stronger SUNY program. The CUNY system, especially for STEM, is in serious disarray.
City college used to be a top engineering school but it's not anymore. If you want to study engineering in New York, I recommend suny Buffalo or maritime
Not in a good area of NYC. Grove is an evil institution, you might benefit from making a sacrifice to go to a better school in the SUNY system for engineering. The CUNY system is disorganized

You are almost guaranteed to be given a midterm within a handful of days of the final exam season. So, you may have 4, 5 exams in the span of a couple weeks. This is frankly ridiculous and poor planning from the professors involved. Academics should be rigorous because of the material, not because professors barely recognize you as a human being.

Students can't simply register for classes on their own; you often have to request permission every semester. Because of this, many required classes filled up immediately, making it hard to stay on track. This leadsto students taking much longer than 4years to finish. grove school was extremely disappointing. disorganized, stressful, not supportive

Many professors seemed disconnected from students' learning needs. Several exams felt unrelated to the textbook or material taught, which made it very difficult to prepare. It often felt like instructors created their own exams without clear departmental guidelines, and the focus was heavily on theory & proofs rather than practical problem-solving.

based on my experience, I wouldn't recommend the engineering program at CCNY. I felt I would have saved time, money, and stress by choosing a different major or school. I hope the program improves in the future, but I can only speak to what I went through.
